concerns.text: Task T019 mandates implementing 'Multilayer Modularity Optimization (MMO)' using 'leidenalg', but the spec's FR-003 explicitly requires the 'Louvain algorithm'. This is a semantic weakening of the functional requirement; the task delivers a different algorithm than the spec demands without a spec amendment.

Task T011 instructs fetching data from 'OpenNeuro ds001734', but the spec's FR-001 and US-1 explicitly mandate 'ds000224'. The task silently relaxes the data source constraint defined in the spec, violating the requirement for dataset-variable fit.

Task T012 describes motion scrubbing and regression but omits the specific requirement from FR-008 to 'regress out 6 rigid-body motion parameters, their temporal derivatives, and the mean FD value using Ordinary Least Squares (OLS)'. The task description is too vague to guarantee the specific OLS implementation required by the spec.

Task T029 mentions framing findings as 'associational' (FR-007) but fails to include the acceptance criterion from US-3 to 'confirm motion control was applied' in the generated report. This drops a specific verification step required by the spec.
